learning_outcomes.topic: C1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry
outcomes: Understand importance of chemistry and nature of matter
Measure properties of matter and handle uncertainty in measurement
Apply laws of chemical combination and Dalton's atomic theory
Calculate atomic and molecular masses using mole concept
Perform stoichiometric calculations and determine percentage composition
Trace the discovery of sub-atomic particles and evolution of atomic models
Explain Bohr's model for hydrogen atom and its developments
Understand the transition towards the quantum mechanical model

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
learning_outcomes.topic: C3: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
outcomes: Justify the need for classification and trace the genesis of periodic tables
Apply modern periodic law and nomenclature rules (including atomic number > 100)
Correlate electronic configuration with element types (s, p, d, f blocks) and position in table

learning_outcomes.topic: C4: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure
outcomes: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach to understand ionic bonding
Analyze bond parameters and apply VSEPR theory for molecular shapes
Utilize Valence Bond Theory and Molecular Orbital Theory concepts
Define thermodynamic terms and apply calorimetry for measurement of ∆U and ∆H
Calculate enthalpy change for various reaction types
Distinguish between homogeneous and heterogeneous equilibria
Classify redox reactions based on classical ideas and electron transfer

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
learning_outcomes.topic: C8: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
outcomes: Represent structural shapes of organic compounds (tetravalence of carbon)
Classify alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic hydrocarbons
